title: Self Driving Products: Product Signals to Pull Requests — Joshua Snyder, PostHog
summary: PostHog is building an automated pipeline that converts product signals like errors and user session events into pull request fixes using background agents. The approach embeds LLM-generated queries rather than raw signals for semantic clustering, prioritizes actionable signal types, and uses agent iteration to optimize expensive steps into one-shot calls.
writeup: Joshua Snyder of PostHog details an automated pipeline that turns product signals like errors and user session events into pull request fixes using background agents. The system embeds LLM-generated queries rather than raw signals for semantic clustering, prioritizes signal types that are actionable, and uses agent iteration to collapse expensive steps into one-shot calls. The talk matters because it shows a concrete path from observed product behavior to code changes without manual triage at each stage.
